## [00:00 - 02:58]

One of my good friends, Mike Walsh, who's a futurist, has a saying where he says, the pandemic has brought the world of 2030 forward to today. And I love this because it's so true. ## [06:03 - 08:51]

Tommy, did it scare you? Did the fly... You know, the music going quiet and then... I hope that scared the crap out of you. Now, look, why am I showing you all of this? First of all, I'm showing you all of this because I hope that it inspires you somewhat to continually innovate with whatever it is that you do. However, there's another reason why I'm showing this with you. I'm showing with you this because I really believe that learning should be fun. Some weird reason we just believe as adults, all of a sudden learning shouldn't be fun anymore. And it reminds me of a teacher of mine when I was really young. When I was in primary school, I had a teacher who was a relief teacher. His name was Mr. Williams. And Mr. Williams always came into class. He had a ponytail and on top of that, he had a ukulele. And he told so many funny jokes and he'd let us play these tennis ball games in class. And one thing that Mr. Williams did when I was young was that he made me love learning. And when Mr. Williams left as a relief teacher, I slowly but surely I fell out of love with learning. And more so when I got into high school and then even more so as I became an adult, I just completely fell out of love with learning. And the reason for this was because learning stopped becoming fun. And I think universally there's this thing with adults where we just all of a sudden have to get so serious. And I'm not saying that we shouldn't be serious at times when we need to be. But when we're in a learning environment, I really believe that learning should be fun. Another good friend of mine named Scott Stratton who's an incredible marketer, he has this brilliant line where he says, When people laugh, they listen. And when they listen, they learn. This is one of my biggest values as a keynote speaker, as a facilitator, as a trainer, as a teacher. Anytime I teach, I try to bring fun. In a way, I guess my wife's a pharmacist. I love this line from her. She says, You've found a way to make the medicine taste good. And all of this, all of this is so that I can ask you the question because I want to learn from you. How do you make things fun with what you do in your industry, with your work, with your profession? How do you make things fun? Tell me, engage with me. I really want to bring more perspectives and learn more perspectives so we can all learn together and really learn how to harness fun and bring more fun into our lives personally as our lives professionally. So tell me in the comment section below, how do you bring fun into your life? Thank you.
